sections.paragraph:
once מְעָט 2Ch 12:7, pr. a scraping (see the root, No. 1). Hence a little. Construed-

(a) as a substantive, followed by a genit. of the noun מְעַט מַיִם paulum aquœ, a little water, Gen 18:4 24:17 , 43 Gen 24:43. מְעַט אֹכֶל a little food, Gen 43:2 as else put after in the genitive, as מְתֵי מְעַט a few men, Deu 26:5. עֵזֶר מְעַט a little help, Dan 11:34. It is also joined with nouns by apposition. Isa 10:7, גֹּויִם לֹא מְעַט “nations not a little,” i.e. not a few; here, by litotes, many.

(b) as an adverb, a little, Psa 8:6 of time, a little while, for a little, Rth 2:7 Psa 37:10 shortly, presently, Hos 8:10 Hag 2:6 of space, a little, 2Sa 16:1. מְעַט מְעַט peu à peu, little by little, Exo 23:30 Deu 7:22. הַמְעַט מִכֶּם is it but a small thing to you? Num 16:9 Eze 16:20, הַמְעַט מִתַּזְנוּתַיִךְ “was this of thy whoredoms but little?” Rarely-

(c) it stands as an adjective, small, few, Num 13:18 26:54 plur. מְעַמִּים a few, Psa 109:8 Ecc 5:1.

With Caph prefixed.-

(1) nearly, almost, within a little, Gen 26:10 Psa 73:2 119:87 .

(2) shortly, Psa 81:15 94:17 quickly, suddenly, Psa 2:12 Job 32:22. כִּמְעַט שֶׁ· shortly that, for scarcely, Son 3:4 comp. כְּ let. B, No. 3.

(3) i.q. מְעַט but intensive; very little (see כְּ B, No. 4 ), ὅσον ὁλίγον, Pro 10:20 1Ch 16:19, “a few men (even) כִּמְעַט very few;” Psa 105:12 very little, 2Sa 19:37.
